title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
A callback is a function passed as an argument to another function, to be executed after some operation completes. It's the oldest pattern for handling asynchronous operations in JavaScript.

Synchronous callbacks run immediately (e.g., forEach, map).
Asynchronous callbacks run later (e.g., setTimeout, event listeners, old Node.js APIs).

Callback hell: deeply nested callbacks become hard to read and reason about. This is why Promises and async/await were introduced.

Callbacks are still valid and widely used for event listeners and simple one-off async operations.
